event: Portrait of Bodhidharma (Daruma) with calligraphic inscription
symbols: red robe enveloping the figure — monastic renunciation and the fiery intensity of single-minded spiritual pursuit
piercing downcast eyes — concentrated inner awareness and unflinching self-examination
calligraphic inscription — the inseparability of word and image in East Asian religious art, where text and painting form a unified meditation
description: A half-length portrait of a bearded figure wrapped entirely in a voluminous red robe and hood, rendered in bold, fluid brushstrokes on a warm tan silk ground. The face is depicted with intense concentration: heavy brows, deep-set eyes gazing slightly downward, a prominent nose, and a full dark beard. Japanese calligraphic characters are inscribed vertically along the upper right portion of the scroll. The painting style is vigorous and expressive, with the red robe dominating the composition.
